{"key_event":"1910 constitutional normalization after Reyes","reasoning":"After the Thousand Days War and Reyes’s fall, constitutional reform, civilian succession, transport links, coffee revenues, and border settlement restored some state reach and legitimacy, but customs dependence, World War I fiscal shock, uneven administration, and weak public health kept capacity only moderate. Civil society recovered more clearly through reopened party competition, elite-popular constraints on executive overreach, coffee-region economic organization, early labor action, and indigenous mobilization, though Conservative dominance, repression, and the influenza crisis limited the rebound.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":5.1,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":6.0}
